executive_summary: Pre-monsoon drainage assessment reveals critical vulnerabilities in the NDLS zone drainage network. Of 5 primary drainage lines, 2 are blocked and 1 is degraded — total network capacity reduced to 65%. Storm Drain SD-7 is at 48% capacity (critical). Track underpass Section 4 flooded 3 times during 2025 monsoon with identical drainage issues persisting. Urban impermeable surface growth (+12% since 2023) has increased peak runoff velocity by 28%. Monsoon onset estimated: June 25 (45 days). Without intervention, 3 track sections face HIGH flooding probability.
ai_findings: 5 primary drainage lines assessed: 2 operational, 1 degraded, 2 blocked
Storm Drain SD-7 at 48% capacity — single blockage point at junction J-14
Primary Drainage D-1 blocked at 3 points: km 0.8, km 1.4, km 2.1
Impermeable surface area increased 12% since 2023 — runoff coefficient 0.72 (design: 0.58)
Historical flood data: underpass Section 4 flooded 3 times in 2025 monsoon (avg depth: 0.4m)

recommendations.action: Emergency clearing of Storm Drain SD-7 at junction J-14. Deploy mechanical excavation team.
Clear Primary Drainage D-1 at 3 blockage points (km 0.8, 1.4, 2.1). Estimated debris: 45 tonnes.
Install temporary diesel pumps (3×500 L/min) at underpass Section 4.
Rehabilitate Overflow Channel O-1 — repair concrete lining at km 0.6-1.2.
Install real-time water-level sensors at 5 critical points for monsoon early warning.
Commission comprehensive drainage capacity study. Evaluate network expansion for 2027 budget cycle.

infrastructure_warnings: Storm Drain SD-7 at 48% — CRITICAL failure risk during heavy rainfall event
Underpass Section 4 has no backup drainage — single point of failure for track operations
Construction debris detected in D-1 — likely from nearby unauthorized construction activity
